        The World Cup is here, and this massive soccer event is the biggest sports betting event in the world. With the action heading to North America for this year's tournament, you can find the best World Cup odds at all the top sportsbooks around. The 2026 World Cup began June 11 and runs until July 19 with matches spread out across the United States, Mexico, and Canada. The 2026 installment also has an expanded 48-field team, further increasing the available World Cup betting markets. Bet the World Cup at FanDuel Sportsbook, where new users get $350 in bonus bets after wagering $5 for seven days:With so many matches set to take place and 48 teams in the field, interested soccer bettors have no shortage of betting options for the 2026 World Cup. Whether you want to place wagers on key soccer betting markets like specific matches and player props or futures bets on the World Cup winner, group stages and awards, this comprehensive World Cup odds page breaks down everything interested bettors need to know for one of the most exciting international sporting events of the year. World Cup odds 2026There are FIFA World Cup tournament odds available on dozens of outcomes and props for each game, as well as plenty of futures in addition to the tournament winner. Here are just some of the potential 2026 World Cup tournament betting lines available:Overall winner and group winnersTeam top scorersTournament awardsWin/lose/draw on each matchMatch goal total and team goal totalsAnytime goal-scorer, first and last goal-scorerPlayer shots, assists, tackles, fouls and disciplineWhen considering World Cup wagering strategies, be aware that on game wagers, three-way money line World Cup odds cover 90 minutes plus stoppage time. If the match goes to extra time, the draw is paid out as a winning bet regardless of the final outcome. Bettors can also place a Draw No Bet wager, where they pick a team to win, but a draw makes the wager a push.Bet the World Cup at FanDuel Sportsbook, where new users get $350 in bonus bets after betting $5 for seven days:Odds to win the World CupThe 2026 World Cup champion will be crowned at MetLife Stadium on July 19, and defending champion Argentina is right in the mix of favorites. The usual standout teams are all in strong form, with Spain, France, England and Brazil also having a real shot at the coveted World Cup trophy. The favorites(Odds via FanDuel as of June 29)France (+330)France has completed group play with three convincing wins over Senegal, Iraq, and Norway. French superstar Kylian Mbappe has played as advertised, scoring four goals in the group stage. Their Round of 32 match will be against Sweden where France will be heavily favored. French manager Didier Deschamps will be back with the squad for their next match after briefly returning to France following the death of his mother.
Argentina (+420)Argentina has dominated all of its opponents so far, with Lionel Messi to thank. His hat trick to open the tournament was the spark Argentina was looking for, and his encore two goal performance against a tougher opponent in Austria has built a wave of momentum for the defending champions. After tacking on another against Jordan to bump his World Cup total up to six, they now sit directly behind France in the overall tournament projections, and Messi seems to be on the right track (-105) to win his first ever World Cup Golden Boot. 
Spain (+650)Spain has reminded everyone of who they are following two straight wins against Saudi Arabia and Uruguay, not conceding a goal in either fixture. With Lamine Yamal getting healthier, Spain will have another opportunity to turn any remaining doubters into believers again as they take on Austria in the Round of 32. Austria will be a solid opponent, but Spain's talent should be overwhelming. Lamine Yamal is +125 to score anytime. 
England (+700)England found some momentum as they concluded their group play, beating Panama 2-0. Harry Kane and Jude Bellingham looked to be in form, which is key for the English squad. England has an excellent opportunity to show fans and bettors that they have what it takes to win in the knockout rounds. England drew Congo DR for their first knockout game, a good pull for the English. Harry Kane is +220 to be the first goal scorer. 
Portugal (+1500)After a disappointing 0-0 draw with Colombia to finish group play, Cristiano Ronaldo and Portugal hope to prove the markets right and handle Croatia in the Round of 32. Ronaldo has looked hot and cold so far, but reminded fans that he still has gas in the tank when he scored his first two goals of the tournament against Uzbekistan. 
The contendersBrazil (+950)Brazil got a boost with the return of superstar Neymar Jr. in their match with Scotland. While absent from the scoresheet, his presence helped propel the team into the Round of 32. Other stars were on display however, Vinicius Junior shined with a two goal performance. He now has four goals in the tournament, and has scored in each of Brazil's fixtures so far. Brazil's thrilling win against Japan has officially pushed them through to the Round of 16.Germany (+1500)Germany continues to fly under the radar at this World Cup, sitting behind many of the top favorites to win it all. Despite this, they have already clinched their group. Germany lost to Ecuador 2-1 to wrap up their group play, but advanced nonetheless.  After opening the tournament with a 7-1 blowout of Curacao, it took a come from behind goal by Deniz Undav to help them secure a victory over Cote D'ivoire. After two straight underwhelming performances, manager Julian Nagelsman will look to stars such as Kai Havertz to shine and easily take care of Paraguay in their first knockout game.
Netherlands (+1900)After easily handling Tunisia, The Netherlands enter the knockout stage as one of the hottest teams in the field. If The Netherlands can continue to play as they did against Sweden and Tunisia, they can contend with the very best and make a deep run. They'll begin in the Round of 32 by facing a strong Morocco squad, in what is sure to be one of the most exciting matches in the round. 
Norway (+3500)Norway finished the group stage falling to France 4-1. The squad was without superstar striker Erling Haaland, and will face the Ivory Coast in the Round of 32. Erling Haaland has been as advertised in his first World Cup so far, he has four goals and doesn't show signs of slowing down. Markets shouldn't punish Norway too harshly for their loss to the current tournament favorites, and still considers them likely to at least advance to the Round of 16.
The longshotsUSA (+3500)The US faced a setback losing in a last second 3-2 disappointment to Turkey to finish group play. Despite this, the squad and manager Mauricio Pochettino remain optimistic and there's no doubt the US remains a dangerous contender in the knockout stage. They drew Bosnia and Herzegovina and will be favored when they kickoff at Levi's Stadium on July 1 at 8 PM E.T. 
Morocco (+4000)Morocco has looked impressive as they worked largely in Brazil's shadow during the group stage, advancing to the Round of 32 as the second place team in Group C. Morocco looks strong defensively and capable of making a run in a second straight tournament. Finishing second means they will face The Netherlands which will be a tall task, but Morocco's grit can keep them in games against any of the top contenders. 
Colmbia (+3300)Colombia upset Portugal in Group K as they snuck ahead of the pre-tournament group favorites and secured first place after a 0-0 draw with Ronaldo and Portugal. Colombia has impressed so far against tough opponents, and will face Ghana on Friday, July 3 at 9:30 E.T in a very winnable Round of 32 match. Columbia is +170 to win by two or more. 
Past FIFA World Cup winnersGermany has the most World Cup final appearances with eight, followed by Brazil with seven, and Italy and Argentina have played for the crown six times. The Netherlands has been a runner-up three times without winning the World Cup, most recently in 2010. Here are the champions, with the years they won the World Cup:Brazil: 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002Germany: 1954, 1974, 1990, 2014Italy: 1934, 1938, 1982, 2006Argentina: 1978, 1986, 2022France: 1998, 2018Uruguay: 1930, 1950England: 1966Spain: 2010World Cup bracket: Knockout stageThe top 32 teams from the group stage have advanced to the knockout stage. The top two teams in each group moved on, along with the best eight third-place teams. The match-ups involving the third-place teams depended on which eight qualified. The final 16 contests are elimination matches.World Cup awards oddsIn addition to the prestige of winning the World Cup, several players will be honored for their individual achievements in the tournament. World Cup odds will be available for the top overall player, leading goal-scorer, best goalkeeper and the young star who makes the biggest impact.Golden Boot oddsThe Golden Boot goes to the tournament's top goal-scorer. If there is a tie, it goes to the player with the most assists. If that does not resolve the tie, the player with the fewest minutes played gets the nod. France's Kylian Mbappe was the 2022 winner, and England's Harry Kane was the 2018 recipient. The favorites (Odds via FanDuel)Lionel Messi (ARG) -105Kylian Mbappe (FRA) +280Ousmane Dembele (FRA) +1200Harry Kane (ENG) +1800Erling Haaland (NOR) +2200Other notables: Vinicius Junior (BRA) +2200; Mikel Oyarzabal (ESP) +3000; Cristiano Ronaldo (POR) +3500;  Jonathan David (BRA) +7000; Ismael Saibari (MAR) +7000; Lamine Yamal (+7000)Golden Ball favoritesThe Golden Ball goes to the player judged to have the best overall performance – more or less the most valuable player of the tournament. Argentina's Lionel Messi has won it twice, including the 2022 tournament. Argentina (three), Brazil (two) and Italy (two) are the only nations with multiple Golden Ball winners since the award became official in 1982. The presumable favorites for this Cup include England's Harry Kane, Spain's Lamine Yamal, and France's Kylian Mbappe.Golden Glove oddsThe Golden Glove is awarded to the tournament's best goalkeeper, as determined by FIFA's expert panel. Argentina's Emiliano Martinez, who had three clean sheets, won the award at Qatar 2022 and also should be among the favorites in the 2026 World Cup sportsbook odds. Spain's Unai Simon and Brazil's Alisson Becker join Martinez atop the list of likely winners.FIFA Young Player Award oddsSince 2006, FIFA has honored the best player in the tournament under the age of 21. Kylian Mbappe (2018) and Argentina's Enzo Fernandez (2022) are the most recent winners. In addition to being a strong candidate for the Golden Ball, Spanish youngster Lamine Yamal stands out as a likely contender for this award.World Cup schedule and key datesThe 2026 World Cup field is set after the final playoff games on March 31.         For the past year, the future of LeBron James has been the subject of speculation. Will the 41-year-old return to the Lakers? Will he go back home to the Cavaliers? Will he retire and end one of the most decorated careers in North American sports history?Kalshi's LeBron James' Next Team market shows another option has emerged as the most likely scenario. As of Monday afternoon, playing for the Golden State Warriors is the favorite in the James' Next Team market, at 48% (Yes: $0.48). That's up from 12.1% on Sunday night.It's also greater than the next most likely scenario: James staying with the Lakers or retiring, which is at 37%. That's down from 46.8% on Sunday night.James' next teamMarket rates at KalshiWarriors48%Lakers or retire37%Cavaliers11%Heat6%Spurs2%Trade on where LeBron will play next season and more NBA markets at Kalshi, where new users get a $15 bonus after making $15 in trades:Why the massive market change? On Monday morning, ESPN's Shams Charania reported that forward Draymond Green will decline his $27.7 million player option for the upcoming season. With that salary off the books or potentially reduced at a later date (note: both James and Green are represented by agent Rich Paul), the Warriors have flexibility under the salary cap to offer James in free agency. They also simultaneously could pursue a trade for Wizards center Anthony Davis.The possibility for a James-Davis-Steph Curry Big 3 in the Bay Area (with Green also an option at a reduced number) could be enough to sway James to move from Southern to Northern California. James, Davis and Curry all played together on the 2024 USA Olympic basketball team that won the gold medal in Paris.James is coming off his worst statistical season since he was a 19-year-old rookie in 2003. He averaged a career-low 20.9 points while averaging just 6.1 rebounds per game (third worst of his career).But he looked reenergized in the playoffs, averaging 23.2 points, 6.7 rebounds and 7.3 assists while shooting 45.9% from the field. He helped a Luka Doncic-less Los Angeles team upset the Rockets in the first round of the postseason before the Lakers were swept by the defending champion Thunder in the Western Conference semifinals.If James, Davis and Curry join forces again, the Warriors would obviously become a major contender in an already loaded Western Conference. Last season Golden State finished with the 10th best record in the West after Jimmy Butler went down with a season-ending ACL injury in January. The Warriors were eliminated by the Suns in the Play-In Tournament.But the prediction markets are jumping on Golden State for next season. At Kalshi, the Warriors have a 6% chance to win next season's NBA title. That's ranks seventh in the NBA and fourth in the Western Conference, behind only the Thunder, Spurs and Timberwolves. The group stage is over, who will teams face in the knockouts once they qualify?

                    at
    3:08 pm ET
                        
                •

        The 2026 World Cup road to the final is finally set as the 48 teams involved in the tournament have been whittled down to 32 following the largest group stage in history. The tournament will now move into the knockout phase, where it's single elimination from here on out. The field consists of the 12 group winners, the 12 runners-up, and the eight best third-placed teams across the 12 groups. It's a fixed bracket, no redraws or swaps so every team in the field knows what their path to the final is and who they'll have to beat to stay alive down through the rounds.The United States men's national team will face Bosnia and Herzegovina on Wednesday July 1, with the winner facing the winner of Belgium vs. Senegal in the round of 16.  Elsewhere in the bracket the round of 32 has a rematch of the 2016 Euro final between Croatia and Portugal, remarkably with stars Cristiano Ronaldo and Luka Modric still leading their teams. Then there's two of the tournaments most exciting upstarts, Norway, led by striker extraordinaire Erling Haaland and Ivory Coast and their teenage sensation Yan Diomande.
Check out the complete bracket below.
